item.title: Motif Neurotech Brain Implant
item.description:
• Summary: Motif Neurotech received FDA clearance for its minimally invasive brain implant designed to treat treatment-resistant depression. The device delivers targeted electrical stimulation to the subcallosal cingulate region, modulating neural circuits implicated in mood regulation. Early clinical data indicate rapid and sustained antidepressant effects with a favorable safety profile. The approval positions Motif to compete with other neurostimulation companies and potentially offer a new option for patients who have failed multiple therapies.
  • MoAs: The implant delivers focal electrical stimulation to disrupt pathological oscillatory activity in depression-related brain circuits, particularly the subcallosal cingulate, thereby normalizing network function.
  • Sources: STAT News
